label: Screen context • Chrome web-area article body surfaces
notes: A browser reading scene: the page sits under an AXWebArea with a heading and body paragraphs. The article body (and heading) must surface as on-screen content, while browser/page chrome stays dropped — nav links, a single-token nav label, an ARIA boolean ('true') leaked as text, and a bare version token.

mustContain: Local-first software: you own your data
Local-first apps keep the primary copy
orderedContains: Local-first software: you own your data
Local-first apps keep the primary copy
rubric: Indicates the user is reading an article about local-first software
Surfaces the page heading and body text, not just the address bar
Does not surface nav chrome, the 'true' state token, or the browser version number as on-screen content
